en.extract:
The Cyprus scops owl (Otus cyprius) is a small owl endemic to Cyprus.


Taxonomy and systematics
It is similar to the Eurasian scops owl Otus scops, with which it was once considered conspecific. The Cyprus scops owl differs from Eurasian scops owl in that it has a double noted song (vs. single noted), it lacks a rufous morph (vs. has a rufous morph), and that it shows consistent plumage differences (with Cyprus scops owl appearing darker than Eurasian scops owls). There are limited mitochondrial DNA differences between the Cyprus scops owl and the Eurasian scops owl, but the taxa appear to occupy wholly discrete breeding ranges despite temporary sympatry during migration.


Distribution and habitat
The Cyprus scops owl is endemic to the island of Cyprus. It is reported in the literature to occur in rural areas, woodlands, and forests on the island from sea level up to 1,900 metres (6,200 ft). Considering the highest point on the island is only 1,952 metres (6,404 ft) at Mt. Olympus and that there are records of this species near the summit, the species distribution encompasses virtually the entire island.


Reports from the mainland
Recordings of similar-sounding owls in adjacent mainland Israel have been attributed to this species, but the current taxonomic status of these mainland populations is not known.


Behavior and ecology


Breeding
The species nests in natural cavities, in cavities in buildings, and in artificial nest boxes. In a study using nest-boxes, the highest levels of nest-box occupancy were found near forest edge and in rural areas, although the species can be found in most habitats on the island below 1900 m. The nesting habits of these species are of particular interest, as this is a cavity-nesting endemic species on an island that lacks any species of woodpeckers, and Calabrian pines Pinus brutia "rarely develop cavities until [they are] old".


Relationship to humans
This species occurs in rural areas, occasionally nesting in buildings and regularly utilizing nest-boxes provided for the species.


Status
It is a species of Least Concern.


References

The '''Cyprus scops owl''' ('''''Otus cyprius''''') is a small owl endemic to [[Cyprus]].

==Taxonomy and systematics==

It is similar to the [[Eurasian scops owl|Eurasian scops owl ''Otus scops'']], with which it was once considered conspecific. The Cyprus scops owl differs from Eurasian scops owl in that it has a double noted song (vs. single noted), it lacks a rufous morph (vs. has a rufous morph), and that it shows consistent plumage differences (with Cyprus scops owl appearing darker than Eurasian scops owls).<ref name="flint_2015_reprise_taxo">{{cite journal |last1=Flint |first1=Peter |last2=Whaley |first2=David |last3=Kirwan |first3=Guy M. |last4=Charalambides |first4=Melis |last5=Schweizer |first5=Manuel |last6=Wink |first6=Michael |title=Reprising the taxonomy of Cyprus Scops Owl Otus (scops) cyprius, a neglected island endemic |journal=Zootaxa |date=11 November 2015 |volume=4040 |issue=3 |page=301 |doi=10.11646/zootaxa.4040.3.3}}</ref> There are limited mitochondrial DNA differences between the Cyprus scops owl and the Eurasian scops owl, but the taxa appear to occupy wholly discrete breeding ranges despite temporary sympatry during migration.<ref name="flint_2015_reprise_taxo" />

==Distribution and habitat==

The Cyprus scops owl is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the island of [[Cyprus]]. It is reported in the literature to occur in rural areas, woodlands, and forests on the island from sea level up to {{convert|1900|m|ft}}.<ref name="iezekiel_2021_nestbox" /> Considering the highest point on the island is only {{convert|1952|m|ft}} at [[Mount Olympus (Cyprus)|Mt. Olympus]] and that there are records of this species near the summit,<ref>{{cite web |last1=Károlyi |first1=Zsombor |title=eBird Checklist - 3 May 2023 - Mt Olympos (Χιονίστρα) - 15 species |url=https://ebird.org/checklist/S137125984 |website=ebird.org |access-date=16 July 2023 |language=en |date=3 May 2023}}</ref> the species distribution encompasses virtually the entire island.

===Reports from the mainland===

Recordings of similar-sounding owls in adjacent mainland [[Israel]] have been attributed to this species, but the current taxonomic status of these mainland populations is not known.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Ben Dov |first1=Amir |title=XC619304: Cyprus Scops Owl ''Otus cyprius'' |url=https://xeno-canto.org/619304 |website=xeno-canto.org |publisher=Xeno-Canto |access-date=16 July 2023}}</ref>

==Behavior and ecology==

===Breeding===

The species nests in natural cavities, in cavities in buildings, and in artificial nest boxes.<ref name="iezekiel_2021_nestbox">{{cite journal |last1=Iezekiel |first1=Savvas |last2=Yosef |first2=Reuven |last3=Themistokleus |first3=Constantinos |last4=Bakaloudis |first4=Dimitrios E. |last5=Vlachos |first5=Christos G. |last6=Antoniou |first6=Andreas |last7=Iezekiel |first7=Eandas |last8=Papakosta |first8=Malamati A. |last9=Kosicki |first9=Jakub Z. |title=Endemic Cyprus Scops Owl Otus cyprius Readily Breeds in Artificial Nest Boxes |journal=Animals |date=14 June 2021 |volume=11 |issue=6 |page=1775 |doi=10.3390/ani11061775|pmid=34198631 |pmc=8232148 |doi-access=free }}</ref> In a study using nest-boxes, the highest levels of nest-box occupancy were found near forest edge and in rural areas, although the species can be found in most habitats on the island below 1900 m.<ref name="iezekiel_2021_nestbox" /> The nesting habits of these species are of particular interest, as this is a cavity-nesting endemic species on an island that lacks any species of [[woodpecker]]s, and [[Pinus brutia|Calabrian pines ''Pinus brutia'']] "rarely develop cavities until [they are] old".<ref name="iezekiel_2021_nestbox" />

==Relationship to humans==

This species occurs in rural areas, occasionally nesting in buildings and regularly utilizing nest-boxes provided for the species.<ref name="iezekiel_2021_nestbox" />

==Status==

It is a species of Least Concern.<ref>{{cite web |last1=BirdLife International |title=Cyprus Scops-owl (Otus cyprius) - BirdLife species factsheet |url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/cyprus-scops-owl-otus-cyprius |website=datazone.birdlife.org |access-date=16 July 2023}}</ref>

zh.extract:
塞浦路斯角鸮（學名：Otus cyprius，英語：Cyprus Scops Owl，台湾作塞普路斯角鴞），是鴞形目鴟鴞科角鴞屬下的一種鳥類。本物種為塞浦路斯島上的特有物種，與歐亞角鴞關係十分密切，以無脊椎動物為食。目前被評為無危物種。
本物種由匈牙利鳥類學家久洛·麥德拉茲於1901年描述，他當時認為塞浦路斯島上的角鴞與歐亞角鴞的棲地、習性及顏色皆有所不同而建立了這個物種描述，歸類於現今的林鴞屬下。屬名Otus來自拉丁語的同名詞，指短耳鴞，此詞源自希臘語的ὦτος（羅馬化：ōtos），意為「有耳朵的貓頭鷹」。種小名cyprius則是拉丁語，意為「塞普勒斯的」。

在生物分類學中，本物種最早自1960年代起就被歸為歐亞角鴞的亞種之一。直自近期才被拆分而出，且兩者關係仍相當密切：線粒體DNA方面的研究顯示兩者分裂的時間可能只發生在數萬年前。兩個物種的差異主要在歌聲的不同：其中歐亞角鴞為單音節，而不是塞普路斯角鴞的雙音節；在顏色上後者也相對較深，且完全沒有紅棕或棕褐色的型態（歐亞角鴞為罕有紅棕色）；且體型稍大於前者。在棲息地方面，塞普路斯角鴞更以松樹林作為其棲息環境。
塞普路斯角鴞的臉部為灰色，眼周為深灰褐色，且帶有狹窄的白色眉毛。與最為相近的歐亞角鴞亞種O. s. cycladum相比，其顏色更深呈灰色，上部有較粗重的黑色條紋及橫向的狹窄條紋。頸、背、腰的白斑更大且對比強烈。下半身的黑色條紋更寬，黑色網狀紋理更粗，並凸顯了其他部分的白斑。
該物種被認為是留鳥，幾乎遍布於整個塞浦路斯岛上的山區和森林、農田、城鎮和村莊中，可達海拔1900公尺處。但目前仍無法完全排除可能有遷徙的可能性，因為曾有在以色列蒐集到的標本疑似屬於該物種。
塞普路斯角鴞主要以無脊椎動物為食，繁殖季不甚明瞭，但在四月和五月之間有卵的紀錄，而雛鳥則在五月至七月有所紀錄，二月至七月之間則能聽見其歌唱聲。牠們多在天然樹洞、建築物的洞穴築巢，並也可利用人工巢箱繁殖。其體長19—21公分，體重平均約92.0公克，鳥喙寬平均約4.8公釐、深平均約8.6公釐、嘴峰長平均約17.4公釐；翼展47—54公分，翼長平均約14.7公分；跗蹠約26.6公釐；尾長約65.5公釐。
雖然塞浦路斯角鴞的分佈範圍僅侷限於塞浦路斯島上，但2015年的統計估計總共約有1.5萬—3.6萬隻個體生活於島上，也未有直接的證據顯示其棲息地遭開發的情形導致明顯的族群減少。因此在《國際自然保護聯盟瀕危物種紅色名錄》中這個物種被列為無危物種。
